The Kremlin welcomed the two-week ceasefire agreement reached between the United States, Israel, and Iran on Wednesday, April 8, describing it as a last-minute deal that pulled the region back from wider war.[1]

signals.headline: Strait of Hormuz Remains Effectively Closed | NO | HIGH confidence**
signals.raw:
Strait of Hormuz Remains Effectively Closed | NO | HIGH confidence**
Despite the ceasefire announcement, ship navigation in the Strait of Hormuz remains effectively suspended, with Maersk stating the ceasefire has yet to provide sufficient security for normal operations.[1]
